Weekly List for John Paul McAdams
Monday, April 20, 2026
Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Lesson 77
Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church)Work for 5 minutes reviewing questions #136-143. Also review the spiritual and corporal works of mercy. Discuss Chapter 19 of OurLife in the Church with your teacher using the discussion questions in Chapter 19 Review in Our Life and the Church Quiz Book (seesyllabus resources). The exercises could be used for additional discussion or to encourage independent work. (Note: one could alsouse the questions in Chapter 19, Lessons 1-4 of the activity book as a guide.)
English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8)Today do workbook pages 154-155. (T.E. pages 314-317.)
Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2)Lesson 8, Selection 39: find the errors and mark them in your book.
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Have someone read you the definition of the Key Words for Lesson 9 and you write down the word. If you had trouble spelling any ofthem, practice.
Science 7 (Exploring the Building Blocks of Science Book 7 Year 1)Read Biology — Chapter 8: Section 8.2: Specialized Cells. Read the remainder of the section. (Page 133 - 136) tomorrow you willanswer questions about it.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America)Read Adam of the Road.
Poetry 8 SyllabusWork on the next 6 lines, up to "... with music." Review the previous lines.Review the poems you learned in Kindergarten (see the Poetry List for Kindergarten to Eighth Grade found at the end of theselesson plans). Recite as many as you can from memory. If you have not already memorized these poems or cannot recite them, thatis fine, just read and enjoy them. 
 
Reading 8 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour. 
 
Tuesday, April 21, 2026
Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Lesson 78
Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church)Work for 5 minutes or less reviewing questions #153-155. Read Chapter 20 of Our Life in the Church. Then discuss Chapter 20 ofOur Life in the Church with your teacher using the "Words to Know"and the discussion questions in Chapter 20 Review in Our Lifeand the Church Quiz Book (see syllabus resources). The exercises could be used for additional discussion or to encourageindependent work. (Note: one could also use the questions in Chapter 20, Lessons 1-4 of the activity book as a guide.)
Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2)Lesson 8, Selection 39: write the selection correctly.
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Have someone read you the definition of the Key Words for Lesson 10 and you write down the word. If you had trouble spelling anyof them, practice.
Science 7 (Exploring the Building Blocks of Science Book 7 Year 1)Re-read Biology — Chapter 8: Section 8.2. Answer The Big Idea and question 8.2b in your workbook.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America)Read Adam of the Road.
Poetry 8 SyllabusMemorize and review for five minutes.
Reading 8 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour. 
 
 
Wednesday, April 22, 2026
Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Lesson 79
Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church)Work for 5 minutes or less reviewing the seven capital sins and the four cardinal virtues. Look up additional information about St.Thomas More.
English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8)Go over workbook pages 156 and 158 with your teacher. Then do workbook pages 157 and 159-160. (T.E. pages 319-327.)
Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2)Lesson 8, Selection 40: find the errors and mark them in your book.
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Do the Lesson 9 & Lesson 10 Review Exercises.
Science 7 (Exploring the Building Blocks of Science Book 7 Year 1)Read Biology — Chapter 8: Section 8.3. Answer question 8.3 and The Big Idea in your workbook.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America)Geography: Complete Chapter 4, Section 5 (Central America and the Caribbean), Section 6 (Costa Rica), and Section 7 (KeyholeMaps of North America) in Eighth Grade Geography.OR If you have The Ultimate Geography and Timeline Guide: Read pp.166-168. Then, using a photocopy of the map on p. 231,identify the countries listed on p. 166, the items listed under ‘Map-It' on p. 168, and the countries listed on the same page.
Poetry 8 SyllabusMemorize and review for five minutes.
Music 8 (Top 100 Classical Music 1685-1928 1-10)Have someone in your house play a short section from The Four Seasons and then from the Haydn string quartet; can you tell whichone it is? Try this exercise several times.
Reading 8 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour. 
 
Thursday, April 23, 2026
Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Lesson 80
Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church)Work for 5 minutes or less reviewing questions #147-148. Discuss St. Thomas More's life. What was the central issue about whichSt. Thomas had to be very clear? After discussing St. Thomas and his life for a little while, write a 20 minute essay about him. This isnot intended to show your writing skills at their very best. It is intended to show what you can write in 20 minutes, about a subject youhave discussed. Set a timer. You might enjoy watching A Man for All Seasons, an excellent movie about St. Thomas More.
English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8)Start today by going over workbook page 161 with your teacher. Then do workbook pages 162-164. (T.E. pages 329-335.)
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Take a spelling test on the Lesson 9 and Lesson 10 Key Words.
Science 7 (Exploring the Building Blocks of Science Book 7 Year 1)Read Biology — Chapter 8: Section 8.4.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America)Read Adam of the Road.
Poetry 8 SyllabusMemorize and review for five minutes, then recite as much of the section as you now know.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2)Read Unit 4, Chapter 17, French Baroque Artists.
Reading 8 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour. 
 
Friday, April 24, 2026
Algebra 1/2 (Saxon 3rd Edition)Test 19
English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8)Go over workbook pages 165-166 with your teacher. Then do workbook page 167. (T.E. pages 336-339.)
Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B)Take Test: Lessons 9 and 10 from Vocabulary From Classical Roots B Test booklet.
History & Geog 8 (Old World & America)Read Adam of the Road.
Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2)Optional: Do Art Pad #17, Night Sky Project, Part 1: Tints and Shades.
Reading 8 SyllabusRead for about 1 hour.

Weekly List for Joseph McAdamsMonday, April 13, 2026Math 87 (Saxon 3rd Edition) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 3Lesson 64Religion 7 (Acts of the Apostles) Syllabus - Week: 15, Day: 4Discuss the questions for Chapter 20, or write out your answers.1. Can you see, in verse 4, a sign of the catholicity of the Catholic Church?2. What miracle did Paul do at Troas?3. What are the important points of Paul’s discourse?Practice defending your faith. Have someone ask you one or more of the questions you have been working on in your FriendlyDefender cards. Answer the question and give the Bible verse that pertains to that answer.English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8) Syllabus - Week: 10, Day: 3Today do workbook pages 86-88. (T.E. pages 162-167.)Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2) Syllabus - Week: 15, Day: 3Review Lessons 1-6, Selection 31: write the selection correctly.Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 1Begin Lesson 9. First look at the meanings of the roots for Lesson 9. Then look at the Lesson 9 Key Words. Which do you knowwithout looking at the definitions? Use the roots to help you. Read over the entries for each of the key words. Add the key words toyour notebook, so that you practice spelling them. Write down definitions for any words you do not already know.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 22, Day: 3Lesson 20. Do the Day 2 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.History & Geog 7 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 14, Day: 2Do the first four of the Questions That Make You Think on pp. 60-61 in The Old World and America. Give the page numbers for thepages on which the answer is to be found. Remember, each answer should be three or more sentences long.Science 6 (Exploring the Building Blocks of Science Book 6) Syllabus - Week: 22, Day: 4Do the Review of Geology — Chapter 16 in your workbook.4/12/26, 8:25 PMPage 1 of 8
   Poetry 8 Syllabus - Week: 12, Day: 4Memorize and review for five minutes, then recite as much of the section as you now know.Music 7 (Alfred's Essentials of Music) Syllabus - Week: 12, Day: 5Listen to Bach's "Christmas Oratario"Reading 7 Syllabus - Week: 18, Day: 1Read for about 1 hour.4/12/26, 8:25 PMPage 2 of 8
     
 Tuesday, April 14, 2026Math 87 (Saxon 3rd Edition) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 4Lesson 65English 8 (Easy Grammar Plus/7-8) Syllabus - Week: 10, Day: 4Go over workbook page 89 with your teacher. Then do workbook pages 90-91. (T.E. pages 168-173.)Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 2Look at the Familiar Words and Challenge Words for each root word in Lesson 9. If there are any you do not know, or can’t figureout, look them up. You can add them to your notebook. Go over any of the Key Words you had trouble spelling.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 22, Day: 4Lesson 20. Do the Day 3 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.History & Geog 7 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 14, Day: 3Pick a topic from the following suggestions, or come up with a topic of your own for a writing assignment (aim for 700 words):What was the Golden Age of Greece?Which are the most important battles in Greek history?Describe the art and architecture of Greece.The Gods of Greece.The Trojan War.A Profile of ...... (any of the important people you studied).Assessment Assignment:Weeks 14-16, Greece PaperReading 7 Syllabus - Week: 18, Day: 2Read for about 1 hour.4/12/26, 8:25 PMPage 3 of 8
        Wednesday, April 15, 2026Math 87 (Saxon 3rd Edition) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 5Test 12Religion 7 (Acts of the Apostles) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 1Read Chapter 21 of Acts. Start to memorize the verses.Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 3Do the Lesson 9 Exercises. Go over any Lesson 9 Key Words you had trouble spelling.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 22, Day: 5Lesson 20. Take Quiz 20.History & Geog 7 (Old World & America) Syllabus - Week: 14, Day: 4Write.Science 6 (Exploring the Building Blocks of Science Book 6) Syllabus - Week: 23, Day: 1Read Geology — Chapter 17: Section 17.1 and 17.2. Answer the questions in your workbook.Poetry 8 Syllabus - Week: 13, Day: 1Work on the next 5 lines, up to “... deeds of mercy;” and review the first lines.Reading 7 Syllabus - Week: 18, Day: 3Read for about 1 hour.4/12/26, 8:25 PMPage 4 of 8

Or, if you prefer, you may write out your answers.1. Did Paul know what awaited him at Jerusalem?2. What advice did those in Jerusalem give him?3. Did this appease those who were angry with Paul?4. What did those men intend to do to Paul? Why didn’t they do it?Review your new cards, both the questions and answers and the Bible verses. (T.E. pages 180-183.)Editing 7 (Editor in Chief, Level 2) Syllabus - Week: 16, Day: 2Review Lessons 1-6, Selection 32: write the selection correctly.Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus - Week: 17, Day: 5Take a spelling test on Lesson 9 Key words. If there are definitions you are unsure of, read them over again.Beginning Latin III Syllabus (LS) - Week: 23, Day: 2Lesson 21. Review first and second declension adjectives (lesson XX).
